title: Phase Transition: Steampunk → Industrial
description: The steampunk world winds down with the elegance of a clock completing its cycle. The party enters a world stripped of beauty and pretense — gray concrete, fluorescent lighting, industrial machinery without the romance of steam. The costumes change: Steamknight becomes Enforcer, Alchemist becomes Lab Technician.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: Three worlds. Three designed authorities. Three sets of Masterites — same archetypes, different costumes. Warden, Arbiter, Tempo, Curator. The pattern is beyond coincidence.

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: The Warden of the Mainspring was the same as the Warden of the Old Guard and the Warden of Routine. Not the same person. The same shape.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: Someone places them and they don't know they've been placed. That's the part that bothers me. Not the placing — the not knowing.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: I kept a complete record. Every NPC conversation from three worlds. Every single one of them had something to say that mattered. I don't think the designer expected anyone to hear all of it.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: The Regulator said the next world has no romance. Just function. Just numbers.

lines: The transformation was not a transformation. There was no portal. No shimmer. No ceremony.
The steampunk world ended and another began, and the seam between them was visible, deliberate, and utterly without apology.

steps.text: The brass became steel. The gears became conveyor belts. The gas lamps became fluorescent tubes. The cobblestones became poured concrete. The romance of steam became the hum of industrial machinery.

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: Armor again. But it's not brass — it's hard plastic. And it has a badge. Says SECURITY.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: The prayers arrived through pipes in the last world. Here they arrive through memos. The words are the same. The delivery is... corporate.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: The formulae have become protocols. Not alchemy — industrial chemistry. Everything documented, everything filed.

lines.speaker: Rogue
lines.text: Lockpicks became access cards. Same principle. Different interface.

lines.speaker: Bard
lines.text: The trumpet became a PA system. I can make it sing if they let me control the frequency.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: Three worlds of costumes. The skills underneath don't change — just the interface. Whoever is designing this understands something about abstraction.

lines.speaker: Fighter
lines.text: Or they understand something about us.
